agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement notification preferences feature. Bird produced two versions of acceptance criteria — v2 supersedes v1 after legal review. The key change: transactional email opt-out (v1 AC2) was removed entirely in v2. Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances. MJ's technical design aligns with Bird's v2.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances (legal requirement)","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled to false must be rejected with HTTP 403 and error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED"},{"rule":"Marketing emails can be opted out of by the user","testable_assertion":"Setting marketing_emails_enabled to false succeeds and suppresses marketing emails for that user"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences for preference updates","implementation_note":"Use PATCH semantics — partial updates only. Validate transactional_emails_enabled cannot be set to false before persisting."},{"decision":"Store marketing opt-out as a boolean field marketing_emails_enabled","implementation_note":"Default to true for new users. Only marketing_emails_enabled is user-mutable."},{"decision":"Return 403 with structured error when transactional opt-out is attempted","implementation_note":"Response body must include error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. This is a domain-level prohibition, not an authorization failure — but 403 is the agreed status code per Bird's v2 AC3."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back in to marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email opt-out is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"403 Forbidden with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED; no state change persisted"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-mandated emails (password resets, security alerts) that cannot be disabled per legal requirement. The field exists in the API schema for explicitness but is immutable by users."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and non-essential communications. User-controlled opt-in/opt-out."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 AC2 allowed transactional email opt-out, but Bird v1 domain rule simultaneously said transactional emails cannot be fully disabled. These were internally inconsistent.","resolution":"Bird v2 resolved this: AC2 was removed entirely after legal review. Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances. v2 is authoritative and supersedes v1."}],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clear and legally grounded","Bird v2 and MJ are fully aligned on transactional email prohibition","API contract (PATCH, 403, error code) is agreed by both Bird and MJ"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether transactional_emails_enabled field should appear in the API schema at all (currently assumed yes, since MJ references it and AC3 validates against it — but an alternative is to simply not expose the field)"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 supersedes v1 as it was submitted later and explicitly reflects legal review","New users default to marketing_emails_enabled = true","The PATCH endpoint requires authentication and authorization (user can only modify their own preferences)"]}}
